ingredients.prep: 14-15 oz (or use another can of diced)

steps.text: Prep: Dice onion, bell pepper, and celery. Mince garlic. Pat shrimp dry and lightly season with a pinch of salt and pepper.

Sweat the trinity: Heat oil and butter in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened and fragrant (avoid heavy browning). Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Bloom tomato paste: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute to deepen flavor.
Build the sauce: Add diced tomatoes, crushed tomatoes, stock, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, paprika, cayenne, black pepper, salt, Worcestershire, and optional hot sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer and thicken: Simmer until the sauce thickens and flavors meld. Stir occasionally. If it gets too thick, add a splash more stock.
Cook shrimp gently: Add shrimp and simmer just until pink and cooked through (avoid boiling hard so shrimp stay tender).
Finish: Stir in lemon juice, most of the green onions, and most of the parsley. Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and hot sauce.
Serve: Spoon shrimp creole over hot cooked rice. Garnish with remaining green onions and parsley.
